question: What is the Cycle to Work scheme and what is the spending limit?
answer: The maximum value you can apply for is £2500. [CH0005] You can only be in one agreement at a time, the next time you will be able to make an application will be once you've repaid your instalments. [CH0007] Using the Cycle to Work scheme for accessories only is a very popular option for employees who already own a bicycle and just want to upgrade the rest of their equipment! [CH0009]

question: How can employees request flexible working?
answer: A formal flexible working request is where you request to contractually change your working arrangements on a permanent basis. [CH0023] Once you have entered the Employee code go through the application process using your Payroll number (labelled Employee ID on your bob profile, or can be found on your payslip.) Once you have completed the application it will be sent to a member of the people team to approve your request. [CH0002] **Trial period** - If you request a flexible working pattern, there’s usually a trial period to assess the feasibility before any final change is confirmed. [CH0024]

question: What private medical insurance does the company provide?
answer: We have introduced BUPA Private Medical Insurance to employees who are Grade 11 (Lead/Principal) and above. [CH0052] This policy will cover the costs of private healthcare from diagnosis through to treatment. [CH0052] This is a taxable benefit and is declarable on a P11D. [CH0052]

question: What is the holiday allowance policy?
answer: * If you leave Made Tech part way through the year your contractual holiday allowance will be pro-rated to account for your leave date. [CH0059] * If a team members performance declines significantly due to abuse of this policy, then we reserve the right to review their use of this policy. [CH0059] In some cases this might mean we'll pay you for accrued and unused holiday and in others it might mean you'll have to pay days taken over your pro-rated allowance back. [CH0059]

question: What does an Associate Software Engineer do?
answer: We don't hire directly for Associate Software Engineer roles at Made Tech. [CH0105] After your first 6 months at Made Tech, you’ll be in a position to pass probation and become an Associate Software Engineer with us. [CH0105] All of our Software Engineers are trained first as an Academy Software Engineer within our 12-week academy programme. [CH0102]

question: What are the responsibilities of a Lead Data Engineer?
answer: You may play one or more roles according to our clients' needs. [CH0141] You will need to be comfortable sharing your knowledge and skills with others. [CH0141] You will also work closely with clients to help shape their data strategy. [CH0141]
